description.en: Construction of 2 modular bicycle garage systems at Marienkirchplatz, each consisting of 3 lockable single garages (total 6 garages). The system must be modular (Baukastensystem) according to DIN 79008 and ADFC-certified. Dimensions: length 2.50m, depth 2.00m, height approx. 1.65m. Support structure made of galvanized, folded steel sheet, material thickness 2mm, statically designed per relevant standards. Facade cladding made of steel sheet, powder-coated RAL 7016 anthracite grey, corrosion protection C3 per DIN EN ISO 12944-2, all ground-contact parts stainless steel. The garages must have a gravel roof and be placed directly on the existing water-bound path surface. Quantity: 2 units.

aiReasoning.en: This is the primary requirement for the bicycle garage structure itself, encompassing all structural and material specifications.

description.en: Supply and install bicycle holder with entry rail made of stainless steel for comfortable guidance of wheels, for tire width 25 to 65 mm, front wheel holder to lock the wheel and prevent rolling out.

aiReasoning.en: This is a specific accessory for securing bicycles inside the garages.

description.en: Drainage via main and emergency drain DN50 made of plastic, downpipe internal, water outlet central and below the support frame.

aiReasoning.en: This requirement addresses the drainage system for the garages.

description.en: Execution includes all ancillary services required for intended use.

aiReasoning.en: This is a general clause covering ancillary services, not a specific technical requirement.

description.en: Supply and install a swiveling LED light strip, battery-operated, with integrated motion sensor and energy-saving timer.

aiReasoning.en: This is a lighting accessory for the garages.

description.en: Coating with anti-graffiti powder PU, DB certification, color RAL 7016 anthracite grey.

aiReasoning.en: This requirement specifies anti-graffiti protection for the garage surfaces.

description.en: Provision of a digital, cloud-based booking platform accessible via smartphone app (iOS/Android) and an operator web portal. The platform must include multi-level AI-based and telephone customer support, without on-site stationary terminal. The app must provide interactive map view, time-tiered pricing, various payment methods (credit card, Klarna, Apple Pay, Google Pay), automatic invoicing, booking confirmation via email, reminders, user account management, and cancellation functions. The operator web portal must allow central management of all parking facilities, device configuration, tariff systems, user management, SSO integration, GDPR-compliant data storage, real-time status display, statistical evaluation, and billing. The system must be hosted on GDPR-compliant servers with encrypted data transmission. The usage fee covers portal and app operation, server maintenance, updates, and support, charged per digital locking cylinder for 60 months. Each single-leaf steel door per single garage must be equipped with a digital battery-operated locking cylinder connected to the booking platform. The door is made of folded galvanized steel sheet, powder-coated like facade, with aluminum handle set with inside lever for emergency opening, door damper. Clear door openings: min. width 0.70m x height 1.45m.

aiReasoning.en: This requirement covers the digital booking system and the associated door locking mechanism, which are integral to the garage operation.

description.en: Supply and installation of laboratory furniture for the laboratory building Itzling of the University of Salzburg. The building consists of a longitudinal 'Research' section (basement, ground floor, 7 upper floors) and a 'Teaching' section (basement, ground floor, 4 upper floors) connected by a bridge. Laboratory areas are not present on all floors, and individual laboratories have different uses (chemical labs, physics labs), which must be considered in the selection of furniture components.

aiReasoning.en: This is the primary requirement for laboratory furniture supply and installation from a different tender.

description.en: Flexibility in the arrangement of shelves and similar elements must be maintained.

aiReasoning.en: This requirement is standalone and specifies the need for adjustable shelving.

deliverableArray.bulletPoint: Locking System for Doors and Drawers

description.en: All lockable doors and drawers must be equipped with cylinder locks, each with 3 keys. Doors up to 900mm height require cylinder backing locks; doors over 900mm require cylinder object turnbuckle locks (usable left/right, turnbuckle diameter 8mm, knob and cylinder replaceable). All lockable elements within a single carcass must use the same key. Retrofit capability must be provided. For underframes with drawers, central locking is optional.

aiReasoning.en: Single requirement covering all locking mechanisms for doors and drawers.

description.en: If required in the tender, doors and single drawers of understructure elements must be lockable with cylinder locks.

aiReasoning.en: Single requirement with no other similar items to merge.

description.en: Modular grid system and dimensions.

aiReasoning.en: Single requirement specifying modular grid system.

deliverableArray.bulletPoint: Work Table Frames and Support Structures

description.en: Work tables must have robust frames with appropriate heights, construction, and assembly. Support frames must have suitable coating and load capacity. For wall-mounted tables, a secure wall connection is required. Tables may be mobile or stationary. Support structures must include leveling capabilities.

aiReasoning.en: Single requirement covering work table frames and support structures.

description.en: Work tables placed in front of or over radiators must ensure operability and air circulation with suitable air slots and revision possibilities. Height differences require vertical cladding made of table top material. Understructures must be designed as variants: fixed base, mobile on 4 rollers, or insert variant (2 rollers, 3-sided base). Body height must allow max. 150 mm from floor to underside of tabletop for constructive features. Depth must correspond to tabletop depth. Height grids must be compatible with each other and with cabinet executions. Cabinet body consists of 2 side panels, a bottom panel, 2 upper traverses, and height-adjustable shelves. Material: double-sided plastic-coated 19 mm thick melamine resin flat pressed board per DIN 68 765 (abrasion resistance S, coating class 2, coating >0.14 mm, emission class E1). Body parts permanently doweled and glued. Shelves and bottom panel for grid widths >600 mm made of directly coated OSB board (22 mm thick). Fronts manufactured in body quality and damped using embedded bump stops. Inner sides of side walls have grid drillings at approx. 32 mm spacing for fittings, shelves, and drawer guides. Front edges of body, fronts, and bottoms provided with rounded PP edge (2-3 mm thick). All other edges require approx. 0.4 mm thick PP thin edge. All edges, even non-visible, treated with deep-penetrating liquid coating in color of adjacent surfaces. Back panel: double-sided lacquered MDF board, 4mm thick, grooved and glued. All shelves must have surface load capacity of at least 30 kg. Each shelf support must have minimum load capacity of 50 kg. Drawers or pull-outs must have minimum load capacity of approx. 30 kg. The substructure must be a load-bearing element with a base firmly connected, made entirely of at least 16 mm thick waterproof glued plywood (DIN 68705) covered with waterproof plastic foil. Substructure back wall removable: for fixed substructures, MDF board painted on both sides, at least 4 mm thick; for mobile substructures, 10 mm thick double-sided plastic-coated melamine resin flat pressed board per DIN 68765 (abrasion resistance S, coating class 2, coating >0.14 mm, emission class E1), glued to body. Base height adapts to roller height (approx. 110 mm). Floor unevenness compensated with 4 leveling feet per substructure. Height corresponds to table working heights (standing/sitting). For special base heights, wastewater pipes routed through base area into media cell, requiring clear base height of 17 cm.

aiReasoning.en: This requirement is standalone and covers all specifications for work tables and understructures, including materials, dimensions, load capacities, and variants. No other items describe the same obligation.

deliverableArray.bulletPoint: Media Cell Structure, Dimensions, Design, and Cladding

description.en: Media cells must have specified structure and dimensions. They must be designed with removable or sliding front panels; for freestanding modules, panels on both sides must be removable. Above work surfaces, media cells must be cladded at least up to the height of the media channel (or the lowest shelf). In the area of sinks in wall work rows, media cells must be executed only up to the height of the media channel upper edge, and an upper horizontal cladding to the wall must be provided. Media cells in the under-table area must be equipped with laterally sliding media covers.

aiReasoning.en: Single requirement covering media cell structure, dimensions, design, and cladding.

description.en: Media outlets must be mountable in large numbers and in any arrangement, and easily accessible. Installation of power sockets for 230V, 400V, and IT in the media cell panel is not permitted.

aiReasoning.en: Single requirement covering media outlet mounting and accessibility.

description.en: A fixed base at the same height as other furniture must be provided towards the floor.

aiReasoning.en: Single requirement for fixed base height.

description.en: Table tops must be suitable for highest demands, executed in uniform thickness using commercially available materials. Standard table tops consist of a carrier board approx. 28mm thick flat pressed board per DIN 68 761, glue V20, emission class E1. The work surface must be covered with at least 0.8mm high-pressure laminate (HPL). Side and front edges must be covered with approx. 3mm thick polypropylene, corners rounded to 3mm radius. Rear longitudinal edge (only in combination with jointing to media carriers) must be finished with 0.4mm melamine resin edge. Underside must be provided with at least 0.03mm synthetic resin foil. Table tops without edge bead must have a total thickness of at least 28mm with impact-resistant rounded safety edge. Table tops with edge bead must have an additional at least 7mm bead, total thickness at edge at least 35mm. If edges are made of different materials, they must bond homogeneously and watertight. All jointing must be executed in the color according to the project's color concept. Additionally, the laboratory worktops must be available in multiple material options: PP table tops with 25mm particle board core and 6mm PP covering; stoneware composite plates with 22mm carrier plate and at least 8mm ceramic covering; phenolic resin solid core plates (e.g., Trespa TopLab Plus or equivalent). All materials must meet specified thicknesses, edge profiles, and joint sealing requirements. Cutouts for sinks must be smoothly glazed. Joints must use waterproof compressible tape and acid-resistant material. Panel joints must be executed as wedge connections to ensure safety against splintering, and the wedge joint must be permanently sealed with a suitable sealant. Front edge must have an upper chamfer and a drip groove on the underside. Basin installation as described under the position 'Sinks and funnel basins'. Worktop surfaces must be acid-resistant and pass chemical resistance tests per DIN 51 102 sheet 2, with physical properties per DECHEMA material table group 71 sheets 1 and 2. Surface must show no staining or loss of gloss after 24-hour exposure to specified chemicals (hydrochloric acid 37%, sulfuric acid 33%, nitric acid 30%, phosphoric acid 85%, acetic acid 99%, chromic acid 60%, sodium chloride 10%, formaldehyde 37%, acetone, ethyl alcohol, dichloromethane, ethyl acetate, trichloroethylene). Test certificates from independent labs must be provided. Additionally, the worktop surface must have a scratch resistance equal to or greater than 4 Newton according to EN 438-2:14. Brochures, durability certificates, and technical documents of the offered panel material must be attached to the offer.

aiReasoning.en: Single requirement covering all laboratory worktop specifications including materials, dimensions, edge profiles, chemical resistance, and scratch resistance.

description.en: Fume hoods must be self-supporting or have a load-bearing steel frame, with working height 900 mm, total depth 900 mm, widths 1200, 1500, 1800, 2100 mm, complying with EN 14175. Understructure prepared for functional elements (chemical storage, liquid collection) and allow installation of safety cabinets and under-counter cabinets, with demountable cladding. Construction must allow future exchange of understructures. Uniform appearance and integrable into other modules. Walk-in fume hoods consist of two side posts, back wall, front panel, fixed window, and two one-piece front sliding panels (horizontally movable independently). Side posts double-walled for media modules and concealed installation. Outer side and back wall: 19 mm directly coated chipboard; inner air guide wall: 5 mm solid core; lining: phenolic resin solid core. Clear access width and interior width deviate minimally from external geometry. Side walls have aerodynamically shaped profiles for display and control elements. Height must allow exhaust connection at 290 cm above finished floor. Front sliding windows vertically movable, easily movable, self-holding at any height, lockable at any height, operable with one hand even beyond 500 mm opening. Handle strip and work surface inflow profile form aerodynamic unit for low-turbulence inflow. Windows connected to counterweights via chemically resistant toothed belts or CNS cables and ball-bearing plastic deflection rollers. Immediately effective fall protection required; passive systems not allowed. Sliding windows must eliminate risk of injury (e.g., crushing). For walk-in fume hoods, front sliding panels horizontal and independently movable, with safety glass for full visibility.

aiReasoning.en: This requirement is standalone and covers the physical construction and dimensions of fume hoods. It is distinct from the air volume control (item 5) and media supply (item 6) for fume hoods.

deliverableArray.bulletPoint: Fume Hood Rod Holders and Rods

description.en: Rod holders for rods (diameter 12-13 mm) must be attached to the back wall of the fume hood, made of material that withstands typical loads. Quantity per fume hood: width 120cm and 150cm - 3 x 3 pieces, width 180cm - 4 x 3 pieces. Per fume hood, deliver and mount: 9 or 12x rod 30mm, 3x horizontal rod (max use of interior width), 3x vertical rod (max use of interior height), 18x rod connectors, resulting in a 3x3 grid approx. 30cm in front of the baffle.

aiReasoning.en: This requirement is standalone and covers rod holders and rods specifically for fume hoods. It is distinct from the general superstructure rod fixtures (item 9) which apply to all laboratory workstations.

description.en: Fume hood lighting requirement (no further details provided in the original data).

aiReasoning.en: Single requirement with no other similar items to merge.

deliverableArray.bulletPoint: Superstructure Attachment and Laboratory Rod Fixtures

description.en: Superstructures (e.g., reagent shelves, shelves, wall cabinets, rod holders, fasteners, media channels, lighting, organizational aids) must be variably attached to the frame profiles of the media cell. Wall cabinets and wall shelves must be provided with labeling fields consisting of a holder into which a label can be inserted and covered with a fixed transparent film. All laboratory workstations must allow attachment of standard laboratory rod material (12-13 mm diameter). Superstructures must be equipped with fixtures to hold rods at the media cell. Every 1.2 linear meters of lab row must have two vertically stacked attachment points for rods (only attachment, not rods). These fixtures must be included in the positions of the respective lab rows, even if not separately listed.

aiReasoning.en: This requirement is standalone and covers general superstructure attachment and rod fixtures for all laboratory workstations. It is distinct from the fume hood-specific rod holders (item 7).

description.en: Stainless steel basins must be made of material number 1.4301, with a sheet thickness of at least 1.0 mm, rust-free, corrosion-resistant, and resistant to disinfectants. The surface must be finely ground and satin-matt brushed. Integration into stainless steel countertops must be seamless, with welds ground smooth and satin-matt brushed. Basins installed from below must be secured against loosening in addition to the sealing joint. All basins must have rounded edges and corners. For countertops made of materials other than polypropylene (PP), basins must be drop-in type with a rolled rim. Clear distance between basin bottom and outlet opening of the fitting must be 50 cm. Eye shower, drip tray, and sanitary fittings must be included in the unit price of the sink. Additionally, basins must have an anti-drumming coating on the underside for thermal and sound insulation, a bottom sloped towards the drain, and must be supplied without overflow but with standpipes. The bottom slope must ensure complete drainage. Any manufacturing-related discoloration of the basin material is considered a rectifiable defect and must be replaced by the contractor at their own cost. Various combinations of countertop and basin materials are listed as options: a) Melamine countertop with stainless steel basin (drop-in with rolled rim); b) Phenolic resin solid core countertop with polypropylene basin (drop-in with rolled rim); c) Polypropylene countertop with polypropylene basin (welded from below); c) Stainless steel countertop with stainless steel basin (seamless welded and ground); d) Corian countertop with Corian basin (seamless welded and ground); e) Stoneware countertop with ceramic basin; f) Composite stoneware countertop with ceramic basin. Polypropylene basins must have a material thickness of at least 10 mm, be deep-drawn, and be welded seamlessly into PP countertops and ground smooth. Stoneware basins must be glued from below, i.e., self-supportingly connected to the table tops, and additionally fixed with two traverses to prevent falling. Basins made of mineral material must be of reference brand Corian or equivalent, and must be seamlessly welded or glued and ground flush with the table tops.

aiReasoning.en: Single requirement with no other similar items to merge.

description.en: Supply and installation of various laboratory cabinets including under-counter cabinets (types R75/60, R77/60, S73/60, S74/90, S74/120, B120 for sink, R73/60, S95/120, S92/90, R93/60, R75/45, S96/120, R74/120, S76/120, R73/45, S92/120, S97/60, S94/120, S95/120, B180 for sink front, B150 for sink front, S94/90), wall-mounted cabinets (type B90 2 doors, type B60 1 door, open shelves, type G5.06.05.01.02 B60 1F, width 90 with 2 doors), hanging cabinets (type B90 2 doors, type B60 1 door), tall cabinets (depth 55 cm, widths 120 cm, 60 cm, 90 cm), safety cabinet (solvent tall cabinet with 2 hinged doors, 4 shelves, floor drip tray, 90 cm width), gas cylinder cabinet for 3 cylinders including 1 CO2 cylinder with pressure reducing station and solenoid valve, glass shelves (2x 2.7 lfm, 2.2 lfm, 7.2 lfm), and a laboratory high cabinet type G5.06.08.01.01 B120 x T55cm. Also include three laboratory wall-mounted cabinets: one width 120 cm with 2 doors, one width 90 cm with 2 doors, and one width 60 cm with 1 door. Additionally, hazardous material cabinets for solvent storage and acid/alkali storage, designed for under fume hood installation: solvent cabinet (140 cm width, 2 pull-out drawers with 1/3 to 2/3 division, type G4.19.16.01.01), acid/alkali cabinet (140 cm width, 2 hinged doors, second pull-out level with spill trays, type G4.19.16.01.02), and solvent safety high cabinet (90 cm width, 2 hinged doors, 4 shelves, bottom spill tray).

aiReasoning.en: All items describe laboratory cabinets and storage solutions from the same tender document, covering various types and configurations.

description.en: Two types of table tops: phenolic resin solid core (3.80 lfm) and stoneware (5.00 lfm).

aiReasoning.en: This item describes table tops for the laboratory tender, distinct from other requirements.

description.en: Provide all required technical documents, test certificates, data sheets, declarations of conformity, and proof of resistance for offered products including fume hoods, extraction devices, safety cabinets, exhaust air scrubber, phenolic resin panels, piping and fittings, sanitary and gas fittings, and weight load capacities. Additionally, provide documents for eyewash stations, waste separation system, liquid containment units, type sheet for laboratory glassware washer, technical documents for laboratory room control and tripod material, and any other documents mentioned in individual descriptions not listed elsewhere.

aiReasoning.en: This requirement covers documentation and certification for the laboratory tender.

deliverableArray.bulletPoint: Submission of Installation and Layout Plans

description.en: The contractor must create and submit installation/layout plans at scale 1:25, including floor plans, elevations, and 3D representations of individual laboratory bench rows, with indication of transfer points for on-site installation. Plans must be submitted no later than 4 weeks after contract award in paper form (A3) and as PDF. Additionally, submit individual plans with position markings for each room.

aiReasoning.en: This requirement is standalone and specifies the planning documentation deliverables.

description.en: Regie work is performed only upon client order. Hours, equipment, transport, and materials must be recorded daily in time sheets and submitted for countersignature. Offered hourly rates include only the wage component; equipment, transport, and material costs are itemized separately. The smallest billing unit is a half hour. Overtime surcharges apply: 50% surcharge equals one-third, 100% surcharge equals two-thirds of the agreed regie price. Rates are provided for different qualification levels: Obermonteure (qualified skilled workers) and Hilfsmonteure (workers with vocational training and experience). Additional regie hours are offered for general components, training, service, and maintenance. Unit price renegotiation due to quantity changes does not apply to regie work.

aiReasoning.en: Single requirement from one source; no merging needed.

description.en: The room is on the 5th upper floor (5.Obergeschoß), identified as 5.OG.10 (10.6), and is of type Afm / Mfm (likely office or meeting room).

aiReasoning.en: Single requirement from one source; no merging needed.

description.en: The laboratory furniture system must have a uniform design, be combinable, and each component must reflect being part of the system. Overall appearance must be aesthetically pleasing and homogeneous in geometries, heights, depths, joint patterns, handles, and colors. The system must be built from components originating from industrial series production, assembled from functional and combinable elements to allow extensive interchangeability. Furniture must be manufactured and adapted to local conditions to achieve a continuous laboratory installation, especially at corners where covers must be provided to avoid hidden corners and angles.

aiReasoning.en: This requirement specifies design and integration standards for the laboratory furniture system.

description.en: The contractor is responsible for the complete media supply within the laboratory furniture, including shut-offs, fittings, drain sets, and connection of all lines to the supply and disposal network. On-site outlets may be from floor, wall, or ceiling; connection points for laboratory rows are shown in plans. For fume hoods, connection points end in the fume hood area. Gas and water lines end with ball valves at approx. 275-285 cm above finished floor in the area above the laboratory row. From the interface point, the contractor must route gas and water lines vertically from above into the laboratory furniture; no bent supply lines are permitted in visible areas. The furniture (support frame, media cell) must be adapted to allow vertical line routing. Media cells and support structures must be adapted to existing outlets, ensuring positioning elements do not lie on or in front of media outlets. Media cells should be executed with as few joints as possible and may extend beyond under-counter and over-counter dimensions. Line material for gas supply must be as specified in the item 'Gasversorgung'.

aiReasoning.en: This requirement is standalone and covers all aspects of media supply and line connections, including routing, adaptation, and material specifications.

description.en: Media supply systems for electrical, sanitary, and gas supply suspended from ceiling via suitable constructions, ensuring maximum flexibility for arrangement of equipment and tables. Systems must be visually and technically analogous to other media supply systems. All required substructures, suspension devices, and transfers are included. Media cell for wall or double workbench, with modular design and possibility for extension for superstructures. For double workbench, splitting into two separate media cells is not required in principle but may be indicated in individual items. Specified in bill of quantities in linear meter-specific quantity. Installation of media lines in existing laboratory benches, with lines concealed where possible and visible lines routed in a visually appealing manner. Required adaptation work on media channels or existing furniture is included.

aiReasoning.en: This requirement is standalone and covers the general media supply systems. It is distinct from fume hood media supply (item 6) which is specific to fume hoods.

deliverableArray.bulletPoint: Fume Hood Media Supply and Connections

description.en: Media cells behind fume cupboards must ensure necessary media supply for cupboards and adjoining workbenches, and are part of the fume cupboard. Gas and water connections must be installed per bill of quantities. Media supply must be analogous to laboratory work cells. Connection to building-side media supply and disposal lines, as well as internal piping, must be carried out by the contractor. If multiple fume cupboards are arranged side by side or integrated into a furniture row, only one common electrical supply point is provided by the building; internal wiring is by the contractor.

aiReasoning.en: This requirement is standalone and covers the media supply specific to fume hoods. It is distinct from the general media supply systems (item 1) and other fume hood requirements.

description.en: Media channels must be made of powder-coated steel sheet or anodised aluminium with a snap-on front cover at least 150 mm high, closed at ends by side covers. They must include freely positionable sockets for power (230V, 400V, IT) and low voltage, with optional extraction fittings for liquid and gaseous media. Designed for future retrofitting, with space reservation for routing media lines and installing extraction points (protection class IP 44). Adaptation work (extension/reduction of take-off points) must be easily performable on site, with connections via cable and plug connectors. A fuse box with transparent cover for RCDs and MCBs must be installed. Low-voltage cabling must be routed separately to avoid contact with power cabling. Low-voltage empty conduits are included in the supplier's scope. The supplier must perform cabling in the channel up to the empty conduit, observing bending radii. Electrical channels must be completely wired including RCD (type G, short-time delayed, surge-proof up to 3kA, pre-fusable) and MCB (characteristic B), with a maximum of six Schuko sockets for 230V (16A/B) per circuit and individual protection for 400V sockets (16 or 32A/B). Power supply of take-off points via cable and plug connectors to allow expandability without rewiring.

aiReasoning.en: This requirement is standalone and covers the specifications for media channels. It is distinct from the general media supply systems (item 1) which are broader.

description.en: A ceiling-suspended media supply system for electrical, IT, and other necessary media connections. Media supply is from the ceiling and must be routed from the suspended ceiling into the supply system. Vibrations of the supply system must be excluded by design. Retrofitting with different media at a later point in time must be possible. Media connections and geometric dimensions must comply with the bill of quantities. The contractor must calculate, manufacture, and pre-assemble the substructure for mounting the media column to the raw ceiling. The ceiling anchor must be made of powder-coated solid steel profile, allowing ceiling fastening and mechanically detachable reception of the function carrier. The substructure cost must be included in the price. Installation must occur before suspended ceilings, with at least three weeks' notice of the installation period. The media pendant must allow media accessibility from both sides. The function carrier must be made of powder-coated, perforated steel profile approximately 130mm wide, with telescopic height adjustment, accommodating media channels on both sides at the lower end. Detachable mounting of brackets for reagent shelves, equipment shelves, and stand holders must be possible between the top edge of the media channel and the bottom edge of the ceiling. The offer must include a technical description and mounting drawing of the media column including substructure. One media channel on each side for installation of electrical, IT, sanitary, and gas extraction points, along with a supply shaft for all media.

aiReasoning.en: Single requirement with no other similar items to merge.

deliverableArray.bulletPoint: Quality, Environmental Compliance, and Color Selection

description.en: Laboratory furniture must be offered in the required quality, manufactured according to industrial standards and the current state of the art. Compliance with all guidelines regarding the use of environmentally friendly materials in manufacturing and later disposal must be ensured. Components must be optimized with regard to energy efficiency and operating costs. Furniture color must be offered and calculated based on the manufacturer's standard color chart, with the client freely choosing one color per furniture component. Standard color palettes for different components must be included in the offer. The furniture base must be black.

aiReasoning.en: This requirement is standalone and covers quality, environmental compliance, and color selection.

deliverableArray.bulletPoint: Connection of Metallic Furniture Parts to Potential Equalization

description.en: Each furniture assembly must be connected by the contractor to the existing potential equalization connection line on the construction side. Metallic furniture parts must be conductively connected to each other, especially components connected to the exhaust air duct such as safety cabinets and fume hoods.

aiReasoning.en: This requirement is standalone and covers electrical safety through potential equalization.

deliverableArray.bulletPoint: Power and IT Cable Routing and Laying

description.en: The contractor must perform routing and laying of power and low-current cables (including hangers, cable ducts, etc.) from interfaces to the connection point in the fuse box of the media channel.

aiReasoning.en: Single requirement covering power and IT cable routing and laying.

deliverableArray.bulletPoint: Coordination with HVAC and Electrical Contractors

description.en: The contractor is responsible for coordinating with the HVAC contractor for connecting ventilation ducts using polypropylene (PP) pipes of appropriate dimensions, with welded or socket connections (no adhesive tape), flexible hoses only in exceptional cases with minimal length, regular fastening to walls or ceilings, and permanent flow direction labels. Also coordinate with the electrical contractor for color specification of Schuko sockets on emergency power supply. All coordination is the contractor's responsibility.

aiReasoning.en: This requirement is standalone and details coordination responsibilities with other trades.

description.en: Commissioning includes all necessary measurements according to ÖVE 8001 (if required, together with electrical contractor, coordination by contractor). Adjustment and measurement of all exhaust-relevant components with documented proof of required air volumes (if necessary, together with HVAC contractor). Perform joint system test, commissioning, certificate creation and acceptance under the leadership of the HVAC contractor, with coordination by the laboratory furniture contractor. Documentation must be handed over at latest during commissioning, including acceptance/handover certificates, measurement protocols, as-built plans, assembly/execution plans, terminal diagrams, circuit diagrams, and other technical documentation. Provide a plant logbook in accordance with ÖVE 8001, coordinated with the electrical contractor's plant logbook. Provide operation and maintenance documents, parts lists, spare parts lists, factory test protocols, installation certificates, training protocol for operating personnel, and operating manuals with complete description and technical data. All documents must be submitted in German, in triplicate, in folders and on data carrier, adhering to the client's specified format. Provide proof of existing test marks for all equipment and components.

aiReasoning.en: This requirement is standalone and covers all commissioning, testing, and documentation obligations.

description.en: Within 3 days after legally binding contract award, the contractor must name a technical officer with necessary commercial powers to ensure professional and timely execution. If required by local construction management, the technical officer must be present on site upon request.

aiReasoning.en: This requirement is standalone and specifies the need for a designated technical officer.

description.en: Provide satisfactory references demonstrating ability to deliver similar projects to a high quality (25% evaluation weight).

description.en: A Town Council representative will monitor the work carried out on a regular basis, and any concerns will be reported to the Town Clerk who will agree an appropriate course of action with the Contractor. Any variations to the contract will be notified to the Contractor by the Town Clerk and agreed in writing between the two parties.

description.en: Use local resources and skills, with a 20% evaluation weight.

description.en: Contractors may submit tenders for 1-year, 2-year, or 3-year service agreements.

description.en: On-site measurements are only permissible for services whose scope and location cannot be graphically represented. Unit prices must include production, delivery, assembly, including transport on site, and installation up to ready-for-operation handover according to execution specifications.

aiReasoning.en: This requirement is standalone and defines the basis for measurement and pricing.

description.en: Carcasses and panels must be made of wood-based materials, specifically multi-layer flat pressed boards with coating class 2 (coating thickness >0.14mm). Panel thickness for carcass plates must be at least 19mm (including coating), and for other panels, back walls, etc., thickness must meet load-bearing capacity standards. Coated panels must have a minimum thickness of 5mm, except back panels of roller bases which must be like body panels. Sight panels must be manufactured like body parts. All wood parts must comply with emission class E1.

aiReasoning.en: This requirement is standalone and specifies the material and thickness requirements for wood-based panels.

deliverableArray.bulletPoint: Structural Integrity and Safety of Connections

description.en: All connections (welds, screws, plug connections, etc.) must be burr-free, easy to clean, and without risk of injury. Sharp edges, hole edges, and outer corners must be deburred. Spot-welded connections are not permitted. Butt joints must be welded and cleanly ground. Cross-sections and material thicknesses must withstand typical loads of laboratory operations.

aiReasoning.en: Single requirement covering structural safety and quality of connections.

deliverableArray.bulletPoint: Fume Hood Containment Performance and Robustness

description.en: The fume hood must meet containment performance per DIN EN 14175-3:2003. Mean value jR,max(SF6) ≤ 0.65 ppm (section 5.3). Peak values for SF6 measurement peaks ≤ 1.95 ppm (3 × 0.65 ppm) in phases 2 and 4, and ≤ 3.25 ppm (5 × 0.65 ppm) in phases 3 and 5. It must pass the robustness test per DIN EN 14175-3:2003 section 5.4, with mean value jR,max(SF6) ≤ 0.65 ppm and peak value ≤ 3.25 ppm (5 × 0.65 ppm). Containment performance must be stated with corresponding volume flow rate for inner and outer measurement planes and robustness. Specified maximum detectable gas values must be achieved at a volume flow rate of ≤ 300 m³/h per linear meter of capture width.

aiReasoning.en: Single requirement with no other similar items to merge.

description.en: Comply with all relevant health and safety legislation. Take measures to minimize risk to the public (e.g., tools not left on pavement, vehicles parked without obstruction). Erection, testing, and maintenance must be carried out in accordance with the latest Wiring Regulations (BS 7671) by a competent person. Produce a Health & Safety Information document including Risk Assessment and Method Statement prior to installation.

aiReasoning.en: This item outlines health and safety requirements for the Christmas lights tender, distinct from other requirements.

deliverableArray.bulletPoint: Compressed Gas Cylinder Cabinet Supply and Maintenance

description.en: Supply of 33 compressed gas cylinder cabinets as specified, including maintenance costs for each of the first 10 years, with the total sum included in the overall position. The contract unit is one year. Provide a cost breakdown for each of the 10 years of the contract period.

deliverableArray.bulletPoint: Piping of Water, Gas, and Wastewater

description.en: The contractor must perform complete piping (including connection) of water and gas supply lines to and within the furniture, from defined interfaces to the connection point of the fittings. Also, complete piping of wastewater lines from the basin drain to the junction into the building wastewater line must be performed.

aiReasoning.en: Single requirement covering piping of water, gas, and wastewater.

description.en: The tender requires the supply of the following chemical substances: Tetrahydrofuran, Acridine Orange, Basic Fuchsin, Methylene Blue, and Gentian Violet.

aiReasoning.en: This requirement is standalone and covers the supply of specific chemicals. No other items describe the same obligation.

description.en: Complete piping for media supply (cold/hot water, gas, etc.) must be pre-installed within service units by the laboratory furniture supplier at the factory and documented. Connections are made by the contractor. Gas and water lines end with ball valves at 275-285 cm above finished floor level. Cold and hot water installation must use a TÜV-tested system with pipes per DIN 16892 and plug connectors or welded. Supply lines in visible areas must be fastened to installation cells using C-rails and series clamps, with suspension spacing not exceeding 60 cm. Pipes must be installed strictly vertically and marked with lettering strips. Media supply to rooms generally comes from ceiling or wall; wastewater disposal goes downward, except via small lifting systems for laboratory rows. Connection points for all media must be shown in installation planning with correct dimensions and positions. Adaptations of supporting frame height and media outlet positions may be necessary due to space constraints, to be clarified based on room plans and as-built measurements. Horizontal supply lines must be dimensioned according to standards based on the number of media outlets. Hot and cold water through lines must be insulated with flexible synthetic rubber hoses having at least 13 mm thickness, fire protection class B1 (flame retardant), and thermal conductivity 0.040 W/mK. All fittings must be secured against unintentional rotation, have removable media-compatible spouts per DIN 12 898 with reliable flat gaskets, allow standard threads for device connection, be connected detachably to the piping system, and be acid-resistant with epoxy powder coating in gray RAL 7004. Labeling must be in German per DIN EN 13 792. Fittings at sinks must have generously dimensioned spout lengths covering the sink area; extensions must match the fitting color. Fittings are part of the sink or trough basin and must be considered in the bill of quantities. Front wash fittings for center tables must be special front fittings (mixing battery and cold water). Outlets and connections must be detailed and approved by the client before assembly. Wastewater and drainage connections must connect to the building disposal network with smooth pipe ends (at least DN50). Wastewater connection points in the media cell must not exceed 30 cm above finished floor level. All drains must have odor traps made of polypropylene with easily openable cleaning options. Consumers such as hot water storage tanks and laboratory glass washers must be connected from the angle valve using flexible armored hoses. Deionized water installation must use a TÜV-tested system with pipes per DIN 16892 and plug connectors or welded joints, completely without metal parts in contact with media. Fittings for RO water must be entirely plastic; no shut-off valves may be installed in RO water ring lines. Sanitary fittings must be laboratory fittings in heavy brass per DIN 12 918 with plastic operating elements, color coding per DIN EN 13 792, suitable for pressure-resistant storage tanks, with maintenance-free ceramic cartridges (180° rotation), and epoxy powder coating or plastic jacket surface protection.

aiReasoning.en: Single requirement with no other similar items to merge.

description.en: Water treatment systems must be secured from drinking water by a backflow preventer (pipe separator). For each laboratory line with cold or hot water connection, a pipe separator with controllable intermediate pressure zone (Group B, Type A) according to DIN EN 1717 must be provided to secure up to fluid category 4. The pipe separators must be arranged in the media cell for easy maintenance access, and the installation location must be marked on the media cell panel above the table top. Installation must comply with DIN EN 1717, including all necessary shut-off valves and pipelines (including connection to the wastewater line). In cases where fluid category 5 is expected, pipe interrupters of open design A1 must be provided; these are separately listed as a position in the performance description. A data sheet of the offered pipe interrupter must be attached to the offer.

aiReasoning.en: Single requirement with no other similar items to merge.

deliverableArray.bulletPoint: Lifting Systems with Safety Equipment for Laboratory Drainage

description.en: For drainage of laboratory lines installed under the table of the furniture, a separate socket must be provided in the under-table area, including safety equipment (magnetic valve in cold water supply line, level sensor in lifting system) that interrupts the water supply when the set maximum level is exceeded. The system must be designed for operation under laboratory conditions, completely and ready for operation.

aiReasoning.en: Single requirement with no other similar items to merge.

description.en: Drain pump included with specifications: DN 22, delivery head 100 cm, max power 0.08 kW, drain hose length 170 cm. Powerful circulation pump with flow rate approx. 400 l/min, max power 0.6 kW, max pressure 0.9 bar, housing made of PP, impeller made of PVDF, for effective cleaning and short cycle times.

aiReasoning.en: Single requirement with no other similar items to merge.

description.en: Comprehensive gas supply infrastructure including central supply of argon and nitrogen with gas cylinder cabinet and solenoid valves (230V, normally closed) for each outlet; helium gas supply system comprising 1 helium gas bottle and 1 pressure reducing station; decentral supply of liquid nitrogen; and gas fittings for argon (with extraction nozzle, qty 3), DL (with hose nozzle, qty 5), helium (with extraction nozzle, qty 3), nitrogen (with hose nozzle, qty 3), and oxygen (with sampling points or hose nozzles). Also includes gas fitting, pressure regulator, and hose nozzle for general gas supply. Additionally, compressed air supply for the laboratory, including specific connection types as part of the gas supply systems. Gas fittings for Argon, DL, N2, compressed air, and Oxygen, including hose nozzles, extraction nozzles, quick-release couplings, and extraction regulators. Specific quantities: Argon (3 pcs), DL (2 pcs), N2 (5 pcs), compressed air (3 pcs), Oxygen (3 pcs). Includes item codes GU.09.24.02.01, GU.09.24.09.01, GU.09.24.16.01. Also includes an Oxygen fitting with extraction regulator (2 pcs) for fume hoods. Gas fittings for room UG.21 Mössbauer 3 include Argon with extraction nozzle, compressed air with hose nozzle, and Nitrogen with hose nozzle. Nitrogen supply is central from an outdoor tank. Workstations are equipped with connections for various gases from gas cylinder safety cabinets. Gas lines end with a ball valve at a height of approx. 275-285 cm at supply points. Connection, introduction, and piping in laboratory furniture/cylinder cabinet is the responsibility of the furniture manufacturer. Additionally, gas fittings for Argon, CO2, compressed air, Helium, Hydrogen, Krypton, Nitrogen, and Ammonia with extraction points, throttles, and hose nozzles. Includes gas cylinder cabinet for 4 cylinders with pressure reducing stations and solenoid valves. Media cells and channels provide utilities: compressed air, N2, Ar, H2, O2, cooling water, warm water, laboratory water, deionized water, and electrical outlets (230V, 400V). Specific quantities and configurations as detailed. Also includes gas fittings for argon with extraction nozzle or hose nozzle, compressed air with hose nozzle, nitrogen with sampling point or hose nozzle, and oxygen with sampling point for room 5.OG.07, and media supply for cold water, compressed air, nitrogen, and argon.

aiReasoning.en: All items describe gas supply and fittings for the laboratory, covering various gases, cabinets, and connection types. They are from the same tender document and detail the same overall requirement.

deliverableArray.bulletPoint: Air-Conducting Elements and Exhaust Air Piping

description.en: All air-conducting elements up to the building exhaust air pipe, including complete piping for exhaust air lines of safety cabinets in the substructure of the digestorium. All control technology components such as volume flow controllers, etc.

aiReasoning.en: This requirement is standalone and covers general air-conducting elements. It is distinct from fume hood exhaust systems (item 5) which are specific to fume hoods.

deliverableArray.bulletPoint: Fume Hood Air Volume Control and Exhaust System

description.en: All fume hoods must include a volume flow controller meeting DIN EN 14175 Part 2, with a damper that closes completely when switched off. The controller must achieve 80% of setpoint within 2 seconds, full regulation within 3 seconds, measurement deviation < 5%, and include cyclic self-monitoring and zero-point adjustment. The actuator must have integrated angle detection, positioning time of 2 seconds for 90°, and be made of PP. Measurement range: 100-1500 m³/h, function at back pressure 100-600 Pa. The controller must detect sash position and horizontal sliding opening, with cable break detection. It must allow constant or sash-dependent air volume (continuous, 2-stage, or 3-stage), PC-based parameterization, and switch to constant value control via control panel. If air volume is too low, optical and acoustic alarm per EN 14175 must be emitted. Signal outputs for on/off, collective fault alarm, and continuous window opening status (2V closed, 10V fully open) must be provided for building MSR system. Additionally, each fume hood must include an exhaust air system with exhaust nozzle (approx. DN250) and underbench extraction connection (approx. DN75) for safety cabinets. The exhaust duct must be mounted behind the baffle, ending as a nozzle above the hood; all air-conducting parts must be PP. On-site exhaust duct ends at approx. 2900 mm above finished floor. Exhaust collection duct must be concealed by cladding. The contractor is responsible for all air technical connections, mounting materials, clamps, sealing, and connection to house installation. Underbench extraction must not affect positioning of media. If normal underbench cabinets are used, they must also be connected to underbench extraction.

aiReasoning.en: This requirement is standalone and covers the air volume control and exhaust system for fume hoods. It is distinct from the construction (item 4) and media supply (item 6) for fume hoods.

deliverableArray.bulletPoint: Room Controller with Master Function and Air Change Management

description.en: The room controller (master function) module must comply with laboratory guidelines and DIN 1946 T7, maintaining protective pressure and required air change. It must cyclically capture individual exhaust air volumes of extracted units and form total exhaust air volume. Operating messages, fault messages, damper position, and sliding window position must be digitally transmitted to DDC and commands from DDC to controllers. The system must regulate different room air change rates for corresponding operating states. The module must monitor and control minimum room air change rates and volume flows. If the minimum room air change is not achieved by fume hood minimum air values, the module must determine and forward the minimum air value to fume hoods and/or room exhaust volume flow controllers. If the minimum volume flow/room air change is exceeded by opening a fume hood, other fume hoods or room exhaust volume flow controllers must be set to their minimum. If the minimum room air change rate is still exceeded, the room supply air must be increased. Additionally, the module must monitor the simultaneity of fume hoods (max. exhaust air volume per laboratory room) and signal if the specified maximum exhaust air volume is exceeded. The module must have sensor inputs for temperature sensor (accepting 0-10V or 4-20mA) and room pressure (accepting 2-10V or 4-20mA). It must control room parameters and allow PC-based parameterization. The module must have 2 analog outputs 2-10V as setpoints and receive the actual room temperature value (analog input) from DDC/BMS.

aiReasoning.en: Single requirement with no other similar items to merge.

description.en: Laboratory room control system for fume hoods/digestories, and room climate with relative humidity between 35% and 55%.

aiReasoning.en: This item describes room control and climate requirements for the laboratory tender, distinct from other requirements.

deliverableArray.bulletPoint: Christmas Lights Installation and Lifecycle Management

description.en: Install Christmas lights and features in Broseley town annually, including 23 sets of pea lights, 1 set on the fir tree, 1 set on the tree on the square, features on poles, 'Welcome to Broseley' sign, festoons, string lights, and 2 snow fall column motifs. All anchor points must be tested. Lights must be fully functional prior to installation. Installation testing must begin on 1 November 2026, and lights must be in working order no later than one week prior to the switch-on date of Saturday 28 November 2026. Maintain lights throughout the display period, dismantle after the festive period, and test for safety and functionality. The contractor must be present on 28 November 2026 to manually switch on the lights after a countdown. After switch-on, set automatic timers: on at 4pm, off at 10pm weekdays, and 4pm to 11pm weekends.

aiReasoning.en: This item describes the core installation and lifecycle management of Christmas lights, from the same tender document.

description.en: Remove all lights after the festival period. Lights are to be dismantled and removed as soon as possible after 6 January each year, but no later than 12 January each year.

aiReasoning.en: Single requirement from one source; no merging needed.

description.en: Attend the switch-on event on Saturday 28 November 2026.

aiReasoning.en: Single requirement from one source; no merging needed.

description.en: Tenders must be submitted in writing in a sealed marked envelope addressed to the Town Clerk, clearly marked 'Tender'. Tenders will be opened by the Town Clerk in the presence of at least two councillors after the deadline. No contractor shall contact councillors or staff to encourage or support their tender outside the prescribed process. All invitations to tender are subject to the Town Council's Standing Order number 17 and the terms of the Bribery Act 2010. A copy of the current public liability certificate and Highway Electrical Registration Scheme certificate must be provided. Tenders will be reported to and considered by the Town Council at a properly convened meeting. Submit any other pertinent documents or certificates relevant to the tender. Provide the organization's health and safety policy document. Provide details of proposed LEDs and product warranty information. All items must be costed individually. All tenders received will be considered by the Town Council on 9 June 2026. All tenders will receive a response from the Town Council week commencing 15 June 2026.

aiReasoning.en: This item details the submission process and compliance requirements for the Christmas lights tender, separate from technical installation.

description.en: The offer is only valid if the offer letter (form) is legally signed at the designated place. Do not sign here (on the summary sheet). The page number is 388 of 409.

description.en: Only discounts and/or surcharges listed on the summary sheet (Schlussblatt / Summenblatt) are considered as offered. description.en: Annual maintenance services for fume hoods and safety cabinets, including measurement of exhaust air volume flow, testing of under-bench extraction, checking function indicators, mechanical and structural checks (damage, corrosion, front sash, timing belt, deflection rollers, lubrication), safety/function checks (opening height over 500mm with acoustic/optical signal per EN 14175, filter inspection, media supply/disposal), checking fit and tightness of exhaust air connection, checking position and ease of movement of volume flow controllers (VSR), readjusting if necessary, cleaning, documentation of results, checking door locking, door arrest, thermomechanics, and lubricating if necessary. The maintenance contract must cover fume hoods, safety cabinets, pipe separators, and volume flow controllers according to manufacturer and legal requirements. Remedying defects found is included, with labor and travel time covered; material costs are charged separately. Work during normal operating hours (Mon-Fri 8:00-15:00). Documentation of condition and work performed, coordinated schedule, and checklists required. The maintenance contract is a contingent item (Eventualposition) that can be called upon under specified conditions. If not called off with delivery order, a separate order is required. Costs included in best bidder determination.

aiReasoning.en: This item describes maintenance services for laboratory equipment from the same tender document, distinct from other requirements.

description.en: Cost of all-inclusive call outs during the festival period for lights erected and maintenance checks. Two scheduled run-throughs of the display to check functionality and attend to minor faults. Call out within 24 hours for major faults including complete motif or light string failure, vandalism, storms, or road traffic accident. Additional call out charges must be stated on the schedule. During downtime, all lights and features are to be tested and any minor work such as replacing bulbs and fixing faulty connections is to be undertaken. The contractor must notify BTC immediately of any defective lights and features that require major repair at additional cost or that cannot be repaired.
